codeReview.summary: The reviewed fix feature has no worker handoff, no transcript entry, no benchmark corpus/harness files, and no commit implementing VAL-PERF-001. The only related code change remains the original degradation-mode commit, which still leaves benchmark evidence absent and also still fails to persist degradation summaries where the writer reads them.

issues.description: The only shipped code related to this fix is still the original degradation-mode branch from `beta-large-mr-degradation`. It updates the run to `completed` while clearing `error_code` and `error_detail`, even though `internal/writer/writer.go:594` only emits the explicit degraded partial-coverage summary when `run.ErrorCode == "degradation_mode"` and `run.ErrorDetail` contains that body. Because the benchmark-evidence fix did not ship a correcting commit, the prior benchmark/reporting failure remains unaddressed end-to-end.

No benchmark corpus, harness, or checked-in runtime evidence for `beta-large-mr-benchmark-evidence` exists in the repository. Searches of the repo and git history found no `internal/bench` package, no benchmark fixtures, and no commit or handoff for this feature, so VAL-PERF-001 still cannot be measured or reported.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission tracks `beta-large-mr-benchmark-evidence` as pending with `completedWorkerSessionId: null`, and there is no transcript entry or handoff for the feature, so there is no evidence that the `go-reliability` benchmark-oriented procedure was executed for this fix review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/chris/.factory/missions/e68ebab7-a1ca-41e1-9c85-d3b7d9b36400/features.json shows `beta-large-mr-benchmark-evidence` pending with no worker session, and `/Users/chris/.factory/missions/e68ebab7-a1ca-41e1-9c85-d3b7d9b36400/worker-transcripts.jsonl` contains no record for that feature.

summary: Review failed. There is no implemented fix artifact for `beta-large-mr-benchmark-evidence`: no worker transcript/handoff, no benchmark corpus or runtime evidence in the repository, and no commit beyond the original `beta-large-mr-degradation` change. The prior benchmark/reporting failure therefore remains unresolved.
